data.title: Audit sprint/debug-everything prompt files to verify CLI_user prohibition propagates from SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md
data.description:
## Implementation

After SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md is updated (d6b8-c154), audit the highest-traffic dispatch prompt files that contain `ticket create bug` commands to ensure the prohibition on --tags CLI_user propagates effectively.

Files to audit:
- `plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/task-execution.md`
- `plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/` prompt files containing `ticket create bug`

For each file:
1. Check if it already references SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md (if yes, prohibition propagates via the boundary doc)
2. If it does NOT reference SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md but contains `ticket create bug` examples, add an inline note near the command: "Do NOT use --tags CLI_user — autonomously-created bugs must not carry this tag (see SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md)"

## TDD Requirement

Structural test: add a test case to an existing or new test file that checks the highest-traffic prompt files either reference SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md or contain explicit CLI_user prohibition near ticket create bug commands.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] sprint/task-execution.md either references SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES.md or contains explicit CLI_user prohibition
  Verify: grep -q "SUB-AGENT-BOUNDARIES\|CLI_user" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/task-execution.md
- [ ] Audit complete: all files with `ticket create bug` verified and note added where needed
  Verify: Documented in task checkpoint comment
